message:
fix(fcp-crypto): reject CBOR tags in CwtClaims values, align with fcp-cbor strictness (br-8azf9)

## Why

Bead flywheel_connectors-8azf9 (P1/HIGH). `fcp-cbor` — the project's
canonical CBOR layer — rejects any `Value::Tag(..)` anywhere in the
tree (see `canonicalize_value_in_place` at
crates/fcp-cbor/src/lib.rs:432, which returns
`SerializationError::UnsupportedTag { tag }`). `fcp-crypto`'s
`CwtClaims::from_cbor` at crates/fcp-crypto/src/cose.rs:398 did not
mirror that refusal: it checked that claim KEYS were integers but
stored each claim VALUE verbatim, including tagged values and values
with tags buried inside nested arrays or maps.

The interop split that creates:

  - A token whose CWT claims carry e.g. `Value::Tag(24, ..)` around
    the issuer, or `Value::Array([.., Value::Tag(1, ..)])` inside a
    structured grant, decodes successfully in fcp-crypto and passes
    `CoseToken::verify`.
  - Any downstream component that re-encodes the claim value through
    fcp-cbor (canonical re-encoding for signature transcripts, schema
    validation, or structural audit) hits
    `SerializationError::UnsupportedTag` and rejects the same token.

That's the non-canonical-token admission vector the bead documents:
fcp-crypto becomes a softer gate than the canonical layer, so a
token accepted at the crypto boundary can still be refused
downstream — or worse, round-trip differently depending on which
crate handles it first.

## What

Two changes in fcp-crypto:

  1. New `CryptoError::InvalidCborTag { tag: u64 }` variant in
     crates/fcp-crypto/src/error.rs. Structured (not a message
     string) so callers can match on the exact tag for telemetry
     and pattern-matching, mirroring
     `fcp_cbor::SerializationError::UnsupportedTag`.

  2. `CwtClaims::from_cbor` calls a new recursive walker
     `reject_cbor_tags(&Value)` on every claim value before insertion.
     The walker descends through `Array` and `Map` (both keys and
     values) and surfaces the first `Tag(tag, _)` it finds as
     `CryptoError::InvalidCborTag { tag }`. Tagged claim KEYS were
     already rejected by the existing integer-only gate; this closes
     the parallel gap on VALUES.

## Tests

Two new tests in crates/fcp-crypto/src/cose.rs::tests:

  - `test_cwt_claim_with_cbor_tag_rejected` — three sub-cases:
      (a) claim value is directly `Value::Tag(24, ..)`
      (b) claim value is `Value::Array` containing a `Value::Tag(1, ..)`
      (c) claim value is `Value::Map` whose inner value is a `Tag(42, ..)`
    All three must surface `CryptoError::InvalidCborTag` with the
    correct tag number.
  - `test_cwt_claim_plain_value_accepted` — regression guard across
    the full range of untagged leaf kinds (Text, Integer, Bytes,
    Bool, Null, nested Array, nested Map) so a future tightening
    of the walker can't accidentally over-reject legitimate shapes.

## Verified

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p2-r1 cargo test -p fcp-crypto -p fcp-cbor
  → fcp-crypto lib: 532 pass, 0 fail (incl. 2 new tests)
  → fcp-cbor lib:   460 pass, 0 fail
  → all aux targets green

Closes flywheel_connectors-8azf9.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -119,6 +119,17 @@ pub enum CryptoError {
     /// COSE header placement or cross-field policy violation.
     #[error("header policy violation: {0}")]
     HeaderPolicyViolation(String),
+
+    /// A CBOR value carried a tag at a position where FCP canonical
+    /// encoding forbids tags. Aligns fcp-crypto's CWT claims decode
+    /// with `fcp_cbor::SerializationError::UnsupportedTag` so a token
+    /// accepted by fcp-crypto is also decodable by fcp-cbor
+    /// (flywheel_connectors-8azf9).
+    #[error("invalid CBOR tag {tag} in CWT claims (tags are not permitted)")]
+    InvalidCborTag {
+        /// CBOR tag number that was encountered.
+        tag: u64,
+    },
 }
 
 /// Result type alias for cryptographic operations.
